novel_features.name: Macro dashboard
novel_features.description: Latest value of a curated set of headline U.S. indicators (unemployment, CPI, GDP, fed funds, 10Y, payrolls) in one call.
novel_features.rationale: Existing FRED tools return one series per call; only a local aggregator fans out and assembles a single macro snapshot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs a quick read on the U.S. economy without choosing and fetching individual series IDs.

novel_features.name: Series compare
novel_features.description: Pull observations for multiple series and align them by date into one table or JSON for correlation.
novel_features.rationale: FRED has no multi-series endpoint; aligning N series by date requires a local join across N observation calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when comparing or correlating two or more indicators over the same window.

novel_features.name: Series latest
novel_features.description: The single most recent observation (date + value) for a series, as a one-liner.
novel_features.rationale: Agent-native shortcut that avoids pulling and parsing the full observation history just to read the current value.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you only need the current print of an indicator, not its history.

novel_features.name: Watchlist
novel_features.description: Persist a set of series locally, sync their latest observations into SQLite, and report which ones moved since the last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Detecting what changed between runs requires local state that no single FRED API call provides.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to track a personal set of indicators and surface only the ones that changed.

novel_features.name: Release calendar
novel_features.description: Recent and upcoming economic data release dates within a day window, aggregated across all releases.
novel_features.rationale: Requires time-windowed aggregation over the full release-dates feed that the raw endpoint does not summarize.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to see what economic data is dropping soon without scanning hundreds of releases.

narrative.headline: Every FRED endpoint, plus a local SQLite store, offline search, and macro commands no other FRED tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Search and pull U.S. and global economic time series from the St. Louis Fed's FRED API. Beyond mirroring every endpoint, it adds a macro dashboard, multi-series compare, a latest-value shortcut, a persistent watchlist that reports what changed, and a release calendar — all agent-native with --json and --select.

quickstart.comment: Check the API key and reachability before anything else.
Find a series ID by text.
Snapshot the headline macro indicators in one call.

troubleshoots.symptom: Response is XML instead of JSON
troubleshoots.fix: Pass --file-type json (it is the default; only an explicit override changes it).
Use 'series search <text>' to find the exact series ID; IDs are case-sensitive (e.g. UNRATE, CPIAUCSL).

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or analyst needs U.S. or global macroeconomic time series — unemployment, inflation, GDP, interest rates, and tens of thousands more — programmatically. It is ideal for fetching current indicator values, pulling historical series for analysis, comparing indicators, and tracking a watchlist over time.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for company financials or stock prices — use an equities/SEC tool.
Do not use it for FRED's GeoFRED/Maps regional-choropleth API — this CLI covers the core time-series API.
Do not use it to write or publish data — FRED is read-only.

recipes.title: Current unemployment rate
recipes.explanation: One-liner for the most recent print of any indicator.

recipes.title: Year-over-year CPI inflation
recipes.command: fred-pp-cli series observations CPIAUCSL --units pc1 --sort-order desc --limit 12 --json --select observations.date,observations.value
recipes.explanation: Uses FRED's pc1 transform for YoY percent change and --select to trim the payload.

recipes.title: Compare unemployment and inflation
recipes.explanation: Aligns multiple series by date into one structure for correlation.

recipes.title: This week's data releases
recipes.explanation: Windowed view of upcoming and recent economic releases.
